laws.title: Public Law 4-31 | Stand Your Ground Act
laws.status_basis: Listed in the source manifest under 'Repealed Laws'. The source body itself contains no self-repeal or sunset; it enacts a permanent general criminal-defense scheme (justifiable use of force / castle doctrine). Repeal is external, so status rests on the repealed-laws listing rather than internal text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'Stand Your Ground Act' / S. 31.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Stand Your Ground Act" or simply as S. 31.

provisions.text_summary: Purposes: protect citizens standing their ground from criminals and legal repercussion.
provisions.evidence: It is the purpose of this act to - Protect citizens from armed criminals.

provisions.text_summary: Enactment/effective on passage and signature.
provisions.evidence: This bill shall be enacted once passed through both chambers of Congress and signed by the President

provisions.text_summary: Definitions: law enforcement agency/officer, dwelling, residence, vehicle.
provisions.exact_change: Would define terms for a self-defense/use-of-force chapter.
provisions.evidence: SEC. 101. Definition (a) Law enforcement agency shall be defined as...
provisions.notes: Substantive general law; placement at t50/s1 is wrong subject matter.

provisions.text_summary: Justifiable use of force; no duty to retreat in dwelling/residence.
provisions.evidence: has no duty to retreat and has the right to stand his or her ground

provisions.text_summary: Presumption of reasonable fear on unlawful/forcible entry.
provisions.evidence: A person is presumed to have held a reasonable fear of imminent peril...

provisions.text_summary: Exceptions to the presumption (lawful resident, criminal activity, law enforcement officer).
provisions.evidence: The presumption set forth in section 103 of this title does not apply if:

provisions.text_summary: Presumption that forcible entrant intends unlawful violent act.
provisions.evidence: is presumed to be doing so with the intent to commit an unlawful act involving force or violence.

current_implementation.detail: Note rp-pl004031-codification carries a full-text quotedContent dump and a Trello link, placed at t50/s1 (war/national defense) which is the wrong title - this is a general self-defense/criminal-law enactment properly belonging in title 18. Declared codification is boilerplate; the note substitutes for what should be excluded-from-code text (repealed) or a concise history note.

recommended_actions: Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl004031-codification.
Reclassify as fully-repealed; retain only a concise historical/statutory note (repealed), and correct placement out of t50 to a crimes/self-defense anchor (t18) if any note is kept.

laws.title: Public Law 4-32 | Warrants, Subpoenas, and Summons Act
laws.status_basis: The supplied law.txt is an Executive Order ("EXECUTIVE ORDER") that merely references PL 4-32 as authority. The operative statute text of PL 4-32 is not present in the supplied source, so no lawful codification conclusion about the Act itself can be drawn.

provisions.text_summary: No authentic statutory text recoverable; the supplied file is an executive order referencing Public Law 4-32.
provisions.evidence: EXECUTIVE ORDER ... under Public Law 4-32
provisions.notes: Source-limited; the Act itself is not recoverable from the supplied file.

current_implementation.detail: Note declares 'Current or not wholly repealed' but the underlying source is an Executive Order, not the enacted PL 4-32 statutory text. Any codification note purporting to represent PL 4-32 rests on the wrong document. Trello link present; no dump.

recommended_actions: Do not codify EO 18-4 as PL 4-32 statutory text; retain at most a concise historical note flagging that the supplied source is an executive order referencing the Act, not the Act itself.
Flag source defect: locate the true PL 4-32 enacted text before any codification; remove Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 4-33 | National Archives Act
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally the Act is permanent and general (establishes NARA as an independent agency); no self-repeal or sunset. Repeal is external.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, immediate effective date, authorizes edit/copy of prior amended bills.
provisions.evidence: This piece of legislation shall be referred to as National Archives Act of 2022; This act shall be severable

provisions.text_summary: Establishes the National Archives and Records Administration as an independent federal agency, headed by the Archivist and four Co-Archivists; President nominates with Senate advice/consent.
provisions.exact_change: Establish NARA and the office of Archivist.
provisions.evidence: The National Archives and Records Administration will be established as an independent federal agency.
provisions.notes: General organic law; proper title placement (t44) but should be actual section text if retained, not just a note.

provisions.text_summary: Responsibilities: maintain records of laws, court decisions, elections; maintain Federal Register/CFR.
provisions.evidence: The National Archives and Records Administration will keep an up-to-date record of all laws...; shall maintain the Code of Federal Regulations (Federal Register)

current_implementation.detail: Full-text dump + Trello link present. Title placement (t44) is correct subject-matter, but the note is a full-text dump rather than a concise history note, and no real section text was created. Since the law is repealed, positive-law section text is not required, but the dump/Trello must be removed.

recommended_actions: Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl004033-codification.
Retain a concise historical note at title 44 recording enactment and repeal of the National Archives Act.

laws.title: Public Law 4-34 | An Act to Prevent the Proliferation of Magistrates and Ensure Basic Judicial Competence
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally a permanent general rule (bar-certification requirement for magistrate/judicial appointments); no self-repeal or sunset. Repeal external.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective on passage.
provisions.evidence: referred to as "H.R. 4015, an Act to Prevent the Proliferation of Magistrates...

provisions.text_summary: No person may be appointed a Magistrate (excluding confirmed Judges/Justices) without federal-court certification; appointing officer liable to impeachment.
provisions.evidence: No person shall be appointed as a Magistrate... without first being certified to practice in a federal court
provisions.notes: Placement at t50/s1 (war/defense) is the wrong subject; belongs in judiciary (t28).

provisions.text_summary: Same certification requirement for Judges/Justices; appointing officer liable to impeachment.
provisions.evidence: No person shall be appointed as a Judge or Justice... without first being certified to practice in a federal court

provisions.text_summary: Persons already appointed get ten days to comply or face impeachment/removal.
provisions.evidence: shall have ten days from its passing into law to become in compliance

current_implementation.detail: Full-text dump + Trello link; placed at t50/s1 which is wrong (judiciary matter belongs in t28). Repealed law, so no section text required, but dump/Trello must be cleaned and placement corrected if a note is kept.

recommended_actions: Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl004034-codification.
Move any retained historical note out of t50 to title 28 (judiciary); mark as repealed.

laws.title: Public Law 4-35 | Recommissioning of the United States Ship New Jersey Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Source is unrecoverable: law.txt contains only Google Drive access-denied debris ('Sorry, the owner hasn't given you permission to download this file'). No statutory text is available; no lawful codification conclusion can be drawn.

provisions.text_summary: Text unavailable - source is a Google Drive permission error page. By title, this appears to be a single-vessel recommissioning measure.
provisions.evidence: Sorry, the owner hasn't given you permission to download this file.

current_implementation.detail: Note declares specific status/codification prose despite the source being unrecoverable debris - an unsupported conclusion. No dump. Trello link present. Placement t46 (shipping) is a plausible subject guess but not verifiable from source.

recommended_actions: Recover the true source text before any codification.
Retain only a source-limited historical note (source unrecoverable); remove Trello link. Likely ceremonial/individual - exclude from permanent Code.

laws.title: Public Law 4-36 | Defense Heraldic Services Act
laws.status_basis: Listed under '1st - 10th U.S. Congress' (not repealed). The extracted HTML contains the full operative text. It makes express amendments to Title 10 with no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title; immediate effective date; conflicting legislation null; severability.
provisions.evidence: referred to as the "Defense Heraldic Services Act of 2022"

provisions.text_summary: Amends Chapter 767 of Title 10 by repealing section 7594 in its entirety and reserving it.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal 10 U.S.C. 7594 (Secretary of the Army authorization to furnish heraldic services) in its entirety; mark reserved.
provisions.evidence: Chapter 767 of Title 10... is amended - (1) by repealing section 7594 in its entirety, and rendering it reserved
provisions.notes: Requires actual textual repeal-marking, not merely a note.

provisions.text_summary: Amends Subchapter II of Chapter 48 of Title 10 by adding a new section furnishing heraldic services under the Secretary of Defense.
provisions.exact_change: Insert new section (source labels it '206') at end of subchapter II of ch. 48 with subsections (a) design of flags/insignia/etc. by DoD authority, (b) advising other agencies on heraldry, (c) reimbursement regulations.
provisions.evidence: Subchapter II of Chapter 48 of Title 10... is amended - (1) by adding the new following section at the end thereof: '206. Furnishing of heraldic services'
provisions.notes: Source section number '206' likely a drafting artifact; integrator must place it correctly within ch. 48 subch. II numbering. This is a real textual insertion, NOT a note.

current_implementation.detail: Correct title (t10) and note correctly says 'Operative amendments are integrated where determinable,' but the draft only added a note at t10/s101/h and did NOT perform the express textual amendments (repeal of 7594; insertion of new heraldic-services section in ch. 48 subch. II). Express amendments to numbered Code provisions must be actual textual amendments.

recommended_actions: Perform actual repeal-marking of 10 U.S.C. 7594 (reserve it) per SEC. II.
Insert the new heraldic-services section into Title 10, Chapter 48, Subchapter II per SEC. III with source-credit to PL 4-36; do not rely on the s101/h note.
Remove Trello link from rp-pl004036-codification.

laws.title: Public Law 4-37 | Border Awareness and Naturalization Act
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally a permanent general immigration/naturalization regulatory scheme; no self-repeal or sunset. Repeal external.

provisions.text_summary: Short title (BAN Act / S. 37).
provisions.evidence: cited as the "Border Awareness and Naturalization Act", the "BAN Act"

provisions.text_summary: Purposes: CIS background-check regulations; organizational structure.
provisions.evidence: It is the purpose of this act to - 1. Create regulations for the Citizenship & Immigration Services...

provisions.text_summary: CIS background-check regulations; alternate-account definitions/actions; immigration regulations; CIS organizational structure (Director under DHS, database requirements).
provisions.exact_change: Establish CIS naturalization/background-check procedures and org structure.
provisions.evidence: Citizenship and Immigration Services shall be an agency under the Department of Homeland Security and the head administrator shall be the Director...
provisions.notes: Much is administrative/procedural (Roblox-specific: alt accounts, Discord messaging), of limited codifiability.

provisions.text_summary: Special tagging of accounts flagged for further investigation.
provisions.evidence: SPECIAL TAGGING OF ACCOUNTS.

provisions.text_summary: Immigration closure seven days before elections; discretionary closure for national security.
provisions.evidence: Immigration shall be closed seven days prior to a Congressional or Presidential Election

provisions.text_summary: Denaturalization process via courts on later-found evidence.
provisions.evidence: The Department of Homeland Security or Citizenship and Immigration Services may file a case to the court systems where they will request the removal of citizenship

provisions.text_summary: Enactment on passage and signature.
provisions.evidence: This bill shall be enacted once passed through both chambers of Congress and signed by the President

current_implementation.detail: Full-text dump + Trello link at t8/s1101/i/2. Title 8 subject-matter is correct, but the note is a full dump not a concise history note. Law is repealed, so no positive-law section text required; dump/Trello must be removed.

recommended_actions: Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl004037-codification.
Retain a concise historical note in title 8 (repealed).

laws.title: Public Law 4-38 | White House Protection Act
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally permanent general (Secret Service jurisdiction around the White House); no self-repeal or sunset. Repeal external.

provisions.text_summary: Short title (White House Protection Act / S. 36).
provisions.evidence: cited as the "White House Protection Act" or simply as S. 36

provisions.text_summary: Purpose: broaden Secret Service jurisdiction around White House and protectees.
provisions.evidence: To broaden Secret Services jurisdiction around the White House and protectees.

provisions.text_summary: Secret Service enforcement of roads/sidewalks around White House; arrest/detain/frisk on complex; weapons prohibition on grounds.
provisions.exact_change: Extend USSS jurisdiction and enforcement authority around the White House complex.
provisions.evidence: North Road, West Road, East Road of the White House shall be enforced by United States Secret Service including sidewalks
provisions.notes: Two sections numbered 'SECTION 3' (Actions and Enactment) in source.

provisions.text_summary: Enactment on passage and signature.
provisions.evidence: This bill shall be enacted once passed through both chambers of Congress and signed by the President

current_implementation.detail: Full-text dump + Trello link at t3/s1. Title 3 (President) is a reasonable subject anchor. Repealed law; dump/Trello must be removed; concise history note is all that's warranted.

recommended_actions: Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl004038-codification.
Retain a concise historical note (repealed) at title 3.

laws.title: Public Law 7-39 | Mandatory FLETC Training ACT
laws.status_basis: Expressly repealed by PL 9-57 (also in this batch): 'S.B.1- Mandatory FLETC Training/Public Law 7-39 is repealed, and the provisions of law amended or repealed by that act are restored or revived as if the act had not been enacted.' Also listed under 'Repealed Laws'.

provisions.text_summary: Short title (Mandatory FLETC Training ACT).
provisions.evidence: cited as the "Mandatory FLETC Training ACT"

provisions.text_summary: Purpose: trained/prepared officers to avoid misconduct.
provisions.evidence: The purpose is to make sure we have trained & prepared officers

provisions.text_summary: Mandatory FLETC training for USMS/FBI/DHS; exam requirements (75% pass); FLETC director/staff structure; exam content.
provisions.evidence: All Federal departments below will have to go through FLETC training...
provisions.notes: Because PL 9-57 revives prior law 'as if the act had not been enacted,' no code footprint should remain.

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately on passage/signature.
provisions.evidence: This bill will be in effect immediately upon Passing both Chambers

current_implementation.detail: Full-text dump + Trello link at t50/s1. Law was expressly repealed by PL 9-57 with revival of prior law 'as if not enacted,' yet the note retains a full-text dump. Placement t50 is also a poor subject fit (law enforcement training).

recommended_actions: Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl007039-codification.
Reflect express repeal by PL 9-57 and revival of prior law; keep at most a concise historical note (repealed).

laws.title: Public Law 7-40 | Media Companies Protection Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Expressly repealed by PL 9-53 (also in this batch), SEC. 2: 'Public Law 7-40, the Media Protections Act is repealed.' Also listed under 'Repealed Laws'.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, definition of 'Media Company'; includes anti-supersession clause.
provisions.evidence: cited as the "Media Companies Protection Act of 2022"; This Act shall not be superseded by any newer law unless that law specifically declares its intent to supersede
provisions.notes: Anti-supersession clause; PL 9-53 nonetheless expressly repeals it, satisfying any specificity requirement.

provisions.text_summary: Bars Dept. of Commerce and Labor from suspending business license/media registry except for inactivity (3 weeks) or law violations.
provisions.evidence: the Department of Commerce and Labor is prohibited from suspending any business license or media registry...

provisions.text_summary: New requirements to create media companies (communications server, a manager); prohibits denial for lacking a Roblox group/employees.
provisions.evidence: the Department of Commerce and Labor shall set the new requirements to start a media company

current_implementation.detail: Full-text dump + Trello link at t50/s1 (wrong subject - this is commerce/media, not war/defense). Expressly repealed by PL 9-53; dump/Trello must be removed and only a concise history note kept.

recommended_actions: Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl007040-codification.
Reflect express repeal by PL 9-53; move any retained note out of t50 to a commerce anchor (t15); mark repealed.
